mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or threshold crossing fails to predict direction with accuracy > 55% over 5-year out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer durables and real estate demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference from periods with mixed demand signals.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 50% during post-shock recovery phases, or 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth when macro volatility exceeds the 90th percentile.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth exceeding 3.5 percent year-over-year forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and erodes real corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL YoY growth crosses the 3.5% threshold, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when inflation expectations are anchored below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict 5-day returns with accuracy above 52% over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity and higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the index of industrial production signal weakening manufacturing demand and supply-side contraction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 48% during financial stress periods, or 60-day forward returns show no negative correlation with INDPRO declines when credit spreads exceed 200 basis points.
